channel.title: 2026-04 on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in 2026-04 on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: k8s: Kubernetes v1.36: In-Place Vertical Scaling for Pod-Level Resources Graduates to Beta
item.description:
Kubernetes v1.36 graduates in-place vertical scaling for Pod-level resources to Beta. Running Pods can now have their aggregate .spec.resources updated, with Kubelet deciding per-container whether to apply cgroup changes in place or restart containers based on resizePolicy.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Prefix-scoped access for User Delegation SAS
item.description:
Prefix-scoped access for User Delegation SAS in Azure Blob Storage is now generally available in all Azure regions. It adds a scope between container-level and single-blob access for SAS tokens.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Generally Available: Azure Functions support for Java 25
item.description:
Azure Functions now generally supports Java 25. You can develop and deploy Java 25 apps locally to supported Azure Functions plans on Linux and Windows, including Flex Consumption.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: dotnet: Building an AI-Powered Conference App with .NET’s Composable AI Stack
item.description:
The post describes building ConferencePulse, an AI-powered conference assistant, using .NET’s composable AI stack. It highlights Microsoft.Extensions.AI, DataIngestion, VectorData, Model Context Protocol, and Microsoft Agent Framework as the main components.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: security: What’s new, updated, or recently released in Microsoft Security
item.description:
Microsoft Security published a brief In the Loop roundup page highlighting recently released, updated, or new security items. The provided content does not include any specific product changes or feature details.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio — April update
item.description:
The April 2026 GitHub Copilot update for Visual Studio focuses on agentic workflows. It adds direct cloud agent sessions from the IDE, user-level support for custom agents, and a new Debugger agent for validation.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: Email threat landscape: Q1 2026 trends and insights
item.description:
Microsoft reported that email threats rose in early 2026, driven by credential phishing, QR code phishing, and CAPTCHA-gated campaigns. It also said disruption of the Tycoon2FA phishing platform reduced observed volume by 15% and prompted changes in attacker tactics.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Microsoft Azure Red Hat OpenShift is now available in Austria East
item.description:
Microsoft Azure Red Hat OpenShift is now generally available in the Austria East region. The region provides three availability zones and supports in-country data residency for customers and partners.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: k8s: Kubernetes v1.36: Tiered Memory Protection with Memory QoS
item.description:
Kubernetes v1.36 updates the alpha Memory QoS feature to separate throttling from reservation, add tiered memory protection by Pod QoS class, expose kubelet metrics, and warn on kernels older than 5.9. With the new policy, Guaranteed Pods get memory.min, Burstable Pods get memory.low, and BestEffort Pods get no protection; throttling via memory.high still applies.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Microsoft Agent Framework 1.0
item.description:
Microsoft Agent Framework is now generally available at version 1.0 for .NET and Python. The release includes stable APIs, long-term support, multi-agent orchestration, multi-provider model support, and cross-runtime interoperability via A2A.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: dotnet: Governing MCP tool calls in .NET with the Agent Governance Toolkit
item.description:
The post describes how to govern MCP tool calls in .NET using the Agent Governance Toolkit. It focuses on policy-based access control, security scanning, and response sanitization to reduce risk in AI agent systems.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: VSTest is Removing its Newtonsoft.Json Dependency
item.description:
VSTest will remove its Newtonsoft.Json dependency in .NET 11 and Visual Studio 18.8. The post says it explains who is affected and how to adjust to the change.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: security: 8 best practices for CISOs conducting risk reviews
item.description:
Microsoft Security Blog published a brief post titled “8 best practices for CISOs conducting risk reviews.” The provided excerpt is a short intro that emphasizes proactive security to reduce exposure to threats.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Memory in Foundry Agent Service
item.description:
Foundry Agent Service now has a preview memory feature for managed long-term memory. It also integrates natively with Microsoft Agent Framework and LangGraph, without requiring external databases to provision, scale, or secure.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Public Preview: Container Network Insights Agent
item.description:
Azure has launched a public preview of the Container Network Insights Agent for troubleshooting Kubernetes networking issues. It provides a lightweight, web-based way to inspect and correlate network signals that are otherwise spread across multiple tools.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Connect to Elastic SAN from Windows VM via VM Extension
item.description:
Azure Elastic SAN now supports connecting volumes to Windows virtual machines through the Elastic SAN VM extension in the Azure Portal. This can be done during VM deployment.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: k8s: Kubernetes v1.36: Staleness Mitigation and Observability for Controllers
item.description:
Kubernetes v1.36 adds client-go and kube-controller-manager changes to reduce controller actions based on stale caches, and adds metrics for observing cache freshness. The initial rollout targets DaemonSet, StatefulSet, ReplicaSet, and Job controllers, with feature gates available to disable the behavior.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Microsoft HTTP DDoS Ruleset for Azure WAF on Azure Front Door Premium
item.description:
Microsoft has announced public preview of the HTTP DDoS Ruleset for Azure WAF on Azure Front Door Premium. The feature is intended to help protect against HTTP-layer DDoS attacks, which can bypass traditional static controls.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Cross-region IPAM pool association in Azure Virtual Network Manager
item.description:
Azure Virtual Network Manager now supports cross-region IPAM pool association in generally available form. A single IPAM pool can be associated with virtual networks across multiple Azure regions to simplify IP address space management and reduce configuration errors.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: dotnet: Welcome to SkiaSharp 4.0 Preview 1
item.description:
SkiaSharp 4.0 Preview 1 has been released. The post also notes that Uno Platform has joined as a co-maintainer.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: High-Performance Distributed Caching with .NET and Postgres on Azure
item.description:
The post introduces a .NET walkthrough for adding distributed caching with Postgres on Azure to improve performance, reduce latency, and lower load on downstream systems. It presents current caching best practices and packages them into a reusable application example.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: security: Simplifying AWS defense with Microsoft Sentinel UEBA
item.description:
Microsoft says Sentinel UEBA can help defenders separate normal AWS activity from suspicious behavior by turning raw CloudTrail logs into binary behavioral signals based on user, peer, and device baselines.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: dotnet: Combining API versioning with OpenAPI in .NET 10 applications
item.description:
The post explains how to add API versioning to .NET 10 applications and document those versions with OpenAPI. It focuses on using both together for clearer API management and documentation.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: k8s: Kubernetes v1.36: Mutable Pod Resources for Suspended Jobs (beta)
item.description:
Kubernetes v1.36 promotes Mutable Pod Resources for Suspended Jobs to beta. It lets controllers change CPU, memory, GPU, and other container resource requests/limits in a suspended Job’s pod template before resuming it, without recreating the Job.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Bring your own AI Gateway in Foundry Agent Service
item.description:
Foundry Agent Service now supports bring-your-own-model scenarios. Agents can use AI models deployed outside Foundry, including models behind enterprise AI gateways such as Azure API Management.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Copilot Student GPT-5.3-Codex removal from model picker
item.description:
GitHub is removing GPT-5.3-Codex from the Copilot Student plan’s model picker starting today. The model is still available when using auto model selection.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: Retirement: Prompt Flow in Azure ML and Foundry
item.description:
Prompt Flow in Microsoft Foundry and Azure Machine Learning will be retired on April 20, 2027 and replaced by Microsoft Agent Framework. The notice says the replacement is a single orchestration platform with new capabilities, but the provided excerpt does not include migration details.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Copilot cloud agent starts 20% faster with Actions custom images
item.description:
Copilot cloud agent startup is now over 20% faster. The improvement comes from optimized runner environments built with GitHub Actions custom images.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot code review will start consuming GitHub Actions minutes on June 1, 2026
item.description:
GitHub Copilot code review will begin consuming GitHub Actions minutes starting June 1, 2026. The change applies to code review usage on pull requests.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Notice about upcoming new format for GitHub App installation tokens
item.description:
GitHub will begin a staged rollout on April 27, 2026 to change the format of newly minted GitHub App installation tokens. The update is intended to improve token performance and will happen over several weeks.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Changes to notification retention and archived repository watches
item.description:
GitHub is changing how long notifications are retained and how watches on archived repositories are handled. The rollout is expected to finish over the next few months.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: k8s: Kubernetes v1.36: Fine-Grained Kubelet API Authorization Graduates to GA
item.description:
Kubernetes v1.36 makes fine-grained kubelet API authorization GA. KubeletFineGrainedAuthz is now always enabled, allowing RBAC to target specific kubelet subresources like nodes/metrics and nodes/stats instead of the broad nodes/proxy permission.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Backup for Elastic SAN
item.description:
Azure Backup now supports Elastic SAN in public preview. It can back up and restore Elastic SAN volumes to help protect against accidental deletion, ransomware, and application-related changes.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Hosted Agents in Foundry Agent Service
item.description:
Azure Foundry Agent Service now offers hosted agents in public preview. Each agent session runs in an isolated sandbox with a clean environment, no shared state, and strong compute boundaries.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Foundry Toolkit for Visual Studio Code (formerly AI Toolkit for VS Code)
item.description:
Foundry Toolkit for VS Code is now generally available. It adds VS Code support for Microsoft Agent Framework, with agent creation from templates or GitHub Copilot, local test/debug with visualization and traces, and direct deployment to Foundry.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: WireGuard In‑Transit Encryption for Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS)
item.description:
WireGuard in-transit encryption is now generally available for Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) clusters that use Azure CNI powered by Cilium and Advanced Container Networking Services (ACNS). It provides transparent node-level encryption for traffic in transit.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Support for .NET 8 (LTS) ends on November 10, 2026—upgrade your apps to .NET 10 (LTS)
item.description:
Support for .NET 8 (LTS) ends on November 10, 2026. App Service apps will keep running, but Microsoft will stop providing security updates and customer support for .NET 8.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: GPT-5.5 is generally available for GitHub Copilot
item.description:
GPT-5.5 is now generally available in GitHub Copilot. GitHub says early testing showed the model performs best on complex, multi-step agentic coding tasks and real-world problem solving.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Cascading read replicas in Azure Database for PostgreSQL
item.description:
Cascading read replicas are now generally available in Azure Database for PostgreSQL. You can create replicas from existing replicas, with support for up to 30 replicas across two replication levels.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: CRC Protection
item.description:
Azure Elastic SAN now supports client-enabled CRC-32C checksum verification for volume connections. CRC protection can be turned on when creating a new Elastic SAN or enabled on an existing one.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Inline agent mode in preview and more in GitHub Copilot for JetBrains IDEs
item.description:
GitHub Copilot for JetBrains IDEs adds inline agent mode in preview, along with improvements to Next Edit Suggestions and new controls for approving terminal commands and file edits. The update also introduces global auto-approve and other related enhancements.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot Chat improvements for pull requests
item.description:
GitHub Copilot Chat has added richer context and new capabilities for working with diffs and pull requests. The feature is available when you ask questions about a pull request in Copilot Chat.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Global pull requests dashboard moves to opt-out public preview
item.description:
GitHub is moving the new global pull requests dashboard to an opt-out public preview, so it will be enabled by default for all users. The new dashboard and inbox experience is transitioning from opt-in to opt-out.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: k8s: Kubernetes v1.36: User Namespaces in Kubernetes are finally GA
item.description:
Kubernetes v1.36 makes User Namespaces GA on Linux, allowing Pods to run with hostUsers: false so container root is isolated from host root. The release also relies on ID-mapped mounts to avoid recursive chown on volumes, improving startup performance and preserving on-disk ownership.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Capacity Autoscaling
item.description:
Capacity Autoscaling for Elastic SAN is now generally available. It lets customers set a policy to automatically expand SAN capacity based on usage instead of overprovisioning or managing capacity manually.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Copilot cloud agent fields added to usage metrics
item.description:
GitHub added a new used_copilot_cloud_agent boolean field to the Copilot usage metrics API in user-level reports. It mirrors the existing Copilot coding agent field after the rename to Copilot cloud agent.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Dependabot-based dependency graphs for Python
item.description:
GitHub now uses Dependabot-based data to generate more complete and accurate transitive dependency trees for Python dependency graphs and SBOMs. This change improves coverage for Python projects.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Disable commit comments across your organization
item.description:
GitHub now lets organization owners disable commit comments at the org level, removing the need to change each repository individually. This follows a prior repo-level option to disable commit comments.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: dotnet: What’s new for .NET in Ubuntu 26.04
item.description:
Ubuntu 26.04 ships with .NET 10. The post explains how to install .NET 10 from the Ubuntu archive, use the new -resolute container tags, build Native AOT apps, and install .NET 8 and 9 from the dotnet-backports PPA.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Monitor for Azure Arc-enabled Kubernetes with OpenShift and Azure Red Hat OpenShift
item.description:
Azure Monitor is now generally available for Azure Arc-enabled Kubernetes clusters running OpenShift and Azure Red Hat OpenShift. It extends monitoring for health and performance across Kubernetes infrastructure and dependent applications.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Better debugging with GitHub Copilot on the web
item.description:
GitHub Copilot Chat on github.com now better handles pasted stack traces, helping users identify the root cause of errors faster. The update improves stack trace recognition reliability in the web experience.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: View and manage agent sessions from issues and projects
item.description:
GitHub now lets you view and steer cloud agent sessions directly from issues and projects. This adds visibility into agent activity without leaving the current workflow.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Pausing new self-serve signups for GitHub Copilot Business
item.description:
GitHub is pausing new self-serve signups for GitHub Copilot Business for some organizations. The change is described as part of ongoing efforts to keep the Copilot experience reliable and sustainable.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot for Jira: Our latest enhancements
item.description:
GitHub says it has added new enhancements to the GitHub Copilot cloud agent for Jira integration. The update focuses on making the integration more powerful and customizable, with more control for teams.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Upcoming change to Copilot usage metrics report download URLs
item.description:
GitHub is changing Copilot usage metrics report download links from Azure Front Door domains to a stable GitHub-owned custom domain. The update is intended to improve URL stability.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot code review user counts now aggregate in usage metrics API
item.description:
The Copilot usage metrics API now aggregates Copilot code review active and passive user counts in enterprise and organization usage reports. This follows the launch of active and passive user identification for Copilot code review.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub CLI: Opt-out usage telemetry
item.description:
GitHub CLI v2.91.0 adds pseudonymous usage telemetry by default. The release notes say the data is used to improve the product and is opt-out.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Arc adds SQL Server on Azure Virtual Machines as a migration target
item.description:
Azure Arc migration now supports SQL Server on Azure Virtual Machines as an additional migration target. Arc-enabled SQL Server instances can now be migrated either to Azure SQL Managed Instance or to SQL Server running on Azure infrastructure using the same unified migration flow.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: k8s: SELinux Volume Label Changes goes GA (and likely implications in v1.37)
item.description:
Kubernetes v1.36 introduces stable Pod field spec.securityContext.seLinuxChangePolicy and a new optional selinux-warning-controller to help prepare for a likely v1.37 default-on SELinuxMount change. When SELinux is enabled, mounts can switch from recursive relabeling to mount-time labeling, which improves performance but can break some shared-volume patterns; clusters should audit for conflicts and opt out per Pod with Recursive if needed.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Logical replication slot sync Status metric for Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server
item.description:
Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server now includes a new Azure Monitor metric, logical_replication_slot_sync_status, in public preview. It reports the synchronization status of logical replication slots.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Database for PostgreSQL flexible server in Denmark East
item.description:
Azure Database for PostgreSQL flexible server is now generally available in the Denmark East Azure region. You can deploy new flexible server instances there.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Dynamic data masking with Azure Cosmos DB
item.description:
Azure Cosmos DB now generally supports dynamic data masking, a server-side policy feature that hides sensitive data from nonprivileged users. It is intended to help protect sensitive information from unauthorized access.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: ai: Introducing Toolboxes in Foundry
item.description:
Microsoft Foundry introduces Toolboxes in public preview as a way to curate, configure, and reuse tools across AI agents. It is intended to reduce duplicated tool wiring, authentication, credentials, and integration code across frameworks and runtimes.

  • Source: Microsoft AI Foundry Blog

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Migrating from virtual network-integrated to Private Endpoint–capable network configuration
item.description:
Azure Database for PostgreSQL servers deployed with virtual network integration can now be migrated to a network configuration that supports Private Endpoint connectivity. This is in public preview.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Enhanced Mirroring Azure Database for PostgreSQL in Microsoft Fabric
item.description:
Microsoft Fabric now generally supports enhanced mirroring for Azure Database for PostgreSQL. The update adds support for native PostgreSQL types like JSON and JSONB, improving scalability and operational confidence for mirrored workloads.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Premium SSD v2 for Azure Database for PostgreSQL
item.description:
Premium SSD v2 is now generally available for Azure Database for PostgreSQL flexible server. It is positioned to deliver up to 4x higher IOPS, lower latency, and improved price-performance for I/O-intensive workloads.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: ai: From Local to Production: The Complete Developer Journey for Building, Composing, and Deploying AI Agents
item.description:
Microsoft announces v1.0 of Microsoft Agent Framework and general availability of the Foundry Toolkit for Visual Studio Code. The post frames these releases as part of a workflow for building, composing, and deploying AI agents from local development to production.

  • Source: Microsoft AI Foundry Blog

item.title: ai: Introducing the new hosted agents in Foundry Agent Service: secure, scalable compute built for agents
item.description:
Microsoft introduced hosted agents in Foundry Agent Service, positioned as managed compute for running agent workloads. The post describes the feature as secure and scalable, aimed at agents that perform tasks rather than only answer questions.

  • Source: Microsoft AI Foundry Blog

item.title: security: AI-powered defense for an AI-accelerated threat landscape
item.description:
Microsoft says it is partnering with Anthropic and other industry players to apply leading AI models, along with its platforms and expertise, to convert AI-driven threat discovery into scalable protection.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Bring your own language model key in VS Code now available
item.description:
Copilot Business and Enterprise users can now use bring your own language model key (BYOK) in Visual Studio Code. This lets teams use their own API keys to access supported models in VS Code.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: C++ code intelligence for GitHub Copilot CLI in public preview
item.description:
The Microsoft C++ Language Server is now in public preview for GitHub Copilot CLI. It uses the IntelliSense engine from Visual Studio and VS Code to extend C++ code intelligence in the CLI.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: k8s: Kubernetes v1.36: ハル (Haru)
item.description:
Kubernetes v1.36 (Haru) ships 70 enhancements: 18 stable, 25 beta, and 25 alpha. Highlights include GA for fine-grained kubelet API authorization, user namespaces in pods, external ServiceAccount token signing, SELinux volume label changes, and removal of the gitRepo volume driver; Service.spec.externalIPs is deprecated for removal in v1.43.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Monitor pipeline
item.description:
Azure Monitor pipeline is now generally available. It provides a centralized control point for telemetry ingestion and transformation for secure, high-throughput, enterprise-scale scenarios.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: dotnet: .NET 10.0.7 Out-of-Band Security Update
item.description:
Microsoft released .NET 10.0.7 as an out-of-band security update to address CVE-2026-40372. The update is security-focused and published outside the regular release cycle.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: azure: [In preview] Generally Available: Azure NetApp Files advanced ransomware protection
item.description:
Azure NetApp Files advanced ransomware protection (ANF ARP) is now generally available. It is intended to help detect, respond to, and recover from ransomware threats on cloud volumes.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: k8s: Gateway API v1.5: Moving features to Stable
item.description:
Gateway API v1.5 promotes six features to the Standard channel and updates the release process to a release train model with feature freeze dates. It also includes a v1 bump for ReferenceGrant, plus notes on migration and compatibility for existing Experimental TLSRoute resources.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: github: Deprecation of security-related organization API fields
item.description:
We have deprecated the following fields from the get an organization and update an organization REST API endpoints: advanced_security_enabled_for_new_repositories dependabot_alerts_enabled_for_new_repositories dependabot_security_updates_enabled_for_new_repositories dependency_graph_enabled_for_new_repositories secret_scanning_enabled_for_new_repositories secret_scanning_push_protection_enabled_for_new_repositories secret_scanning_validity_checks_enabled GitHub Code Security configurations now…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: CodeQL now supports sanitizers and validators in models-as-data
item.description:
CodeQL data extensions now support defining custom sanitizers and validators in models-as-data. This adds new ways to model how data is cleaned or checked in code scanning rules.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: Detection strategies across cloud and identities against infiltrating IT workers
item.description:
Microsoft Security published a post on detection strategies for infiltrating IT workers in cloud and identity environments. It frames the topic around increased remote hiring, digital onboarding, and reliance on online identity verification and remote access.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: devops+ai: 🔥Agentic AI Email Bot on AWS Bedrock AgentCore: Building the Thread, Sending the Reply, and Operating in Production (2/2)🔥
item.description:
The article notes that making email-safe HTML is nontrivial and effectively requires treating it as a separate format from normal web HTML.

  • Source: Let’s do DevOps

item.title: dotnet: Writing Node.js addons with .NET Native AOT
item.description:
Microsoft describes replacing a C++ Node.js addon with a C# implementation built with .NET Native AOT, removing the Python build dependency. The post explains how to create a Node.js native addon in C# using N-API, LibraryImport, and UnmanagedCallersOnly.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: github: Changes to GitHub Copilot plans for individuals
item.description:
GitHub is changing Copilot plans for individual users. The post says the changes are intended to support service reliability and sustainability, but the provided excerpt does not include the specific plan details.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Monitor supports native OTLP ingestion using the Azure Monitor Agent
item.description:
Azure Monitor now has public preview support for native OTLP ingestion through the Azure Monitor Agent (AMA). OpenTelemetry-instrumented applications can send telemetry directly to AMA, which exports it to Azure Monitor.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: security: Making opportunistic cyberattacks harder by design
item.description:
Microsoft describes changes to Dynamics 365 and Power Platform aimed at reducing opportunistic attacks by removing credentials, shrinking attack surfaces, and applying platform engineering controls. The post focuses on design choices that make common attack paths harder to exploit.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Sunsetting SHA-1 in HTTPS on GitHub
item.description:
GitHub says it will remove SHA-1 use in HTTPS for GitHub and its CDNs. This change affects browsers and other software that connect to or view GitHub over HTTPS.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: Cross‑tenant helpdesk impersonation to data exfiltration: A human-operated intrusion playbook
item.description:
Microsoft describes a human-operated intrusion pattern that uses external Microsoft Teams collaboration to impersonate IT helpdesk staff, persuade users to grant remote access, then abuse legitimate tools and admin protocols for lateral movement and data exfiltration. Microsoft Defender can detect related activity across Teams, endpoint, and identity telemetry.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot CLI now supports Copilot auto model selection
item.description:
GitHub Copilot CLI now generally supports Copilot auto model selection for all Copilot plans. When enabled, Copilot picks the most efficient model automatically.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally available: Anthropic Claude Opus 4.7 on Azure Databricks
item.description:
Azure Databricks now supports Anthropic Claude Opus 4.7 via Azure Databricks AI Model Serving. The model is available generally and is positioned for complex extraction and agentic reasoning tasks.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Azure Functions runtime v3 on Linux Consumption will stop running September 30, 2026
item.description:
Azure will enforce the retirement of Azure Functions runtime v3 for Linux Consumption–based Function Apps. The runtime was already retired on December 13, 2022, and affected apps will stop running on September 30, 2026.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Configure AKS backup using a single Azure CLI command
item.description:
Azure Backup now supports configuring backup for Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) clusters with a single Azure CLI command. This reduces the manual steps previously required to install and set up the backup extension and related components.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: security: Containing a domain compromise: How predictive shielding shut down lateral movement
item.description:
Microsoft describes a real-world domain compromise where exposure-based containment and predictive shielding were used to slow lateral movement, stop credential abuse, and interrupt the attacker’s progress.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Copilot CLI supports custom registry based MCP allowlists
item.description:
Enterprise and organization administrators can now bring their own MCP registry and enforce allowlist policies in Copilot CLI. This feature is in public preview and brings MCP governance controls to…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: Retirement: Azure Kubernetes Service support for Ubuntu 22.04
item.description:
Azure Kubernetes Service will retire support for Ubuntu 22.04 on June 30, 2027. Users should migrate to Ubuntu 24.04 or later before that date to avoid service disruption.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: ai: What’s New in Microsoft Foundry Fine-Tuning | April 2026
item.description:
April 2026 adds three Reinforcement Fine-Tuning updates in Microsoft Foundry: global training for o4-mini with lower per-token pricing in 12+ regions, new GPT-4.1-based model graders for improved reward signals, and a best-practices guide for RFT workflows.

  • Source: Microsoft AI Foundry Blog

item.title: azure: [In preview] Generally Available: User and group quota reports in Azure NetApp Files
item.description:
Azure NetApp Files now includes user and group quota reporting in preview/GA update. The feature provides visibility into quota limits and usage for NFS, SMB, and dual-protocol volumes.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Manage agent skills with GitHub CLI
item.description:
GitHub CLI now includes gh skill, a new command for discovering and installing agent skills. The post says it is intended to make managing skills easier for AI coding agents.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: Building your cryptographic inventory: A customer strategy for cryptographic posture management
item.description:
Microsoft published guidance on building a cryptographic inventory to improve cryptographic posture management and prepare for quantum-safe migration. The post points to Microsoft Security tools, lifecycle best practices, and partner solutions.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Rule insights dashboard and unified filter bar
item.description:
GitHub announced a new rule insights dashboard and a unified filter bar for repository rulesets. The update is meant to make it easier to spot trends such as blocked-push spikes and bypass patterns.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: Dissecting Sapphire Sleet’s macOS intrusion from lure to compromise
item.description:
Microsoft reports a macOS intrusion campaign attributed to Sapphire Sleet that uses social engineering and user-driven execution to bypass macOS protections. The campaign is described as stealing credentials, cryptocurrency assets, and other sensitive data.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Claude Opus 4.7 is generally available
item.description:
Claude Opus 4.7 is now generally available in GitHub Copilot. GitHub says early testing showed improved multi-step task performance and more reliable agentic execution.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: CodeQL 2.25.2 adds Kotlin 2.3.20 support and other updates
item.description:
CodeQL 2.25.2 has been released. It adds support for Kotlin 2.3.20 and includes other unspecified updates.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Enable Copilot cloud agent via custom properties
item.description:
GitHub Copilot cloud agent can now be enabled selectively for specific organizations using custom properties. Before this change, enterprise admins and AI managers could only turn the agent on or off across all organizations.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: Retirement: End of lift reminder of HBv2/HC-Series/NP-Series Azure Virtual Machine in Azure Batch Pool
item.description:
Azure Batch will retire support for HBv2-series, HC-series, and NP-series VMs in Batch pools on May 31, 2027. The notice applies to these VM sizes only when used in Azure Batch pools.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Encrypt Premium SSD v2 and Ultra Disks with Cross Tenant Customer Managed Keys
item.description:
Cross-tenant customer-managed keys for Premium SSD v2 and Ultra Disks are now generally available. Managed disks can now be encrypted with a CMK stored in an Azure Key Vault in a different Microsoft Entra tenant.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: dotnet: Pin Clustering in .NET MAUI Maps
item.description:
The .NET MAUI Map control in .NET MAUI 11 now supports pin clustering, automatically grouping nearby pins into cluster markers. The post also covers configuring separate clustering groups and handling cluster tap events on Android and iOS.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: security: Incident response for AI: Same fire, different fuel
item.description:
Microsoft published a blog post on incident response for AI, focusing on how existing IR practices still apply while AI-specific telemetry, tools, and skills are needed. It frames AI incidents as similar to traditional response work but with different signals and operational needs.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Site Recovery support for Windows Azure VMs with NVMe disk controllers.
item.description:
Azure Site Recovery now supports Azure-to-Azure replication and disaster recovery for Windows Azure VMs that use NVMe-enabled Generation 2 VM families. Supported examples include Da/Ea/Fa v6-series and Ebsv5/Ebdsv5.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Announcing: Minimum billable object size for cooler storage tiers
item.description:
Azure Blob Storage and ADLS will now use a 128 KiB minimum billable object size for the cool, cold, and archive access tiers. Objects smaller than 128 KiB on those tiers will be billed as 128 KiB.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: OIDC support for Dependabot and code scanning
item.description:
Dependabot and code scanning now support OpenID Connect (OIDC) authentication for private registries configured at the organization level. This removes the need to store long-lived credentials as repository secrets.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Deployment context in repository properties and alerts
item.description:
GitHub added deployment context to repository properties and security alert pages. Two new built-in repository properties, deployable and deployed, now expose this information.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: dotnet: .NET and .NET Framework April 2026 servicing releases updates
item.description:
The post is a brief recap of April 2026 servicing updates for .NET and .NET Framework. It does not include specific version details or change items in the provided text.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: .NET 11 Preview 3 is now available!
item.description:
Microsoft announced .NET 11 Preview 3, covering updates across the runtime, SDK, libraries, ASP.NET Core, .NET MAUI, C#, Entity Framework Core, and container images. The provided excerpt does not include specific feature details.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: github: Link code scanning alerts to GitHub Issues
item.description:
GitHub added the ability to link code scanning alerts to GitHub Issues. The feature is in public preview and is meant to connect security remediation work with existing planning and tracking workflows.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Dependabot and code scanning: Org-level private registries
item.description:
GitHub now lets organizations configure multiple private registries at the org level for Dependabot and code scanning, which is useful for setups that use more than one internal package feed. Previously, org-level settings supported only one private registry per ecosystem.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Secret scanning pattern updates and product improvements
item.description:
GitHub says it is rolling out secret scanning updates that improve detection coverage, APIs, and related workflows. The post says these changes are part of ongoing secret scanning product improvements, but the provided excerpt does not include具体 details.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Smart Tier (Azure Blob and Data Lake Storage)
item.description:
Smart Tier for Azure Blob and Data Lake Storage is now generally available in nearly all zonal public cloud regions. The release excludes Israel Central, Qatar Central, and UAE North at launch.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: SBOM exports are now computed asynchronously
item.description:
SBOM exports from repository dependency graph pages and the new API endpoints are now computed asynchronously. This changes export handling from a synchronous action to an async one.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Model selection for Claude and Codex agents on github.com
item.description:
GitHub.com now lets users select the model when starting Claude and Codex third-party coding agent runs. The feature is available for these agents, similar to Copilot cloud agent.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Code Quality: Improvements to standard findings in public preview
item.description:
GitHub Code Quality has updated its standard findings experience in public preview to make repository-wide navigation and triage easier. The changelog notes improvements focused on finding reliability and related issues, but does not provide detailed feature specifics in the excerpt.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure File Sync in Belgium Central, Malaysia West, and Indonesia Central
item.description:
Azure File Sync is now generally available in Belgium Central, Malaysia West, and Indonesia Central. The service supports tiering data from on-premises Windows Servers to Azure Files for hybrid scenarios and migration.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Copilot data residency in US + EU and FedRAMP compliance now available
item.description:
GitHub Copilot now supports data residency in US and EU regions, keeping inference processing and related data within the selected geography. It also adds FedRAMP compliance for US government customers.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Fix merge conflicts in three clicks with Copilot cloud agent
item.description:
GitHub added a new “Fix with Copilot” button on github.com for resolving merge conflicts, powered by Copilot cloud agent. The feature lets users fix conflicts from a comment flow in three clicks.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Managed identity support for graphical session recording
item.description:
Azure Bastion graphical session recording in public preview now supports managed identities for writing to storage accounts. This removes the need to use other credentials for storage access when configuring session recording.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Storage Mover now available in Azure Government (US)
item.description:
Azure Storage Mover is now generally available in Azure Government (US). It provides a fully managed way for U.S. government customers and partners to migrate large-scale file data into Azure Government cloud environments.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Monitor AKS applications with OpenTelemetry and Azure Monitor
item.description:
Azure Monitor now supports monitoring applications running on Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) with OpenTelemetry. The preview includes autoinstrumentation to deploy the Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry distribution to workloads.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Granular Encryption-in-Transit Controls for SMB and NFS on Azure Files
item.description:
Azure Files now lets you configure encryption-in-transit separately for SMB and NFS at the storage account level. This enables protocol-specific security policies instead of a single shared setting.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Azure Managed Grafana Essential SKU will retire on March 30, 2027
item.description:
Azure Managed Grafana Essential SKU will retire on March 30, 2027. Customers are advised to move to Azure Managed Grafana Standard SKU or Azure Monitor Dashboards with Grafana before that date to avoid service disruption.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Public Preview: StandardV2 NAT Gateway as an outbound type for AKS
item.description:
AKS Public Preview adds StandardV2 NAT Gateway as an outbound type for managed and BYO VNets. It provides zone-redundant outbound connectivity, up to 100 Gbps outbound throughput, and IPv6 outbound support.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Remote control CLI sessions on web and mobile in public preview
item.description:
GitHub Copilot CLI now supports remote control via copilot --remote, allowing users to monitor and steer a running CLI session from web and mobile. The feature is available in public preview.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Enforcing new limits and retiring Opus 4.6 Fast from Copilot Pro+
item.description:
GitHub is enforcing new usage limits for Copilot Pro+ and retiring Opus 4.6 Fast from that plan. The change is intended to address high-concurrency and heavy-usage patterns.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot usage metrics now aggregate Copilot cloud agent active user counts
item.description:
GitHub is renaming Copilot coding agent to Copilot cloud agent and updating the data schema to reflect that change. Copilot usage metrics will now aggregate active user counts for the cloud agent under the new naming.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Pausing new GitHub Copilot Pro trials
item.description:
GitHub is pausing new GitHub Copilot Pro free trials because of increased abuse of the trial system. The change is intended to protect platform integrity for legitimate developers.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Actions workflows are limited to 50 reruns
item.description:
GitHub Actions workflow runs are now limited to 50 reruns per workflow. Attempts to rerun a workflow beyond that limit will fail with a failed check suite.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot CLI activity now included in usage metrics totals and feature breakdowns
item.description:
GitHub Copilot usage metrics now include CLI activity in the overall totals and feature breakdowns. Previously, CLI metrics were only available separately in a standalone totals_by_cli section.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Event Grid as a destination for Stripe events now in Public Preview
item.description:
Azure Event Grid is now in public preview as a destination for Stripe events. Developers can route Stripe events into Event Grid to build event-driven architectures without managing webhook infrastructure or custom brokers.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Copilot cloud agent’s validation tools are now 20% faster
item.description:
GitHub says the validation tools used by Copilot cloud agent are now 20% faster. The tools include CodeQL, the GitHub Advisory Database, secret scanning, and Copilot code review.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: ai: What’s new in Microsoft Foundry | March 2026
item.description:
March 2026 Foundry updates include Agent Service GA with private networking, new GPT-5.4 and GPT-5.4 Mini models, Priority Processing, Phi-4 Reasoning Vision, SDK 2.0 GA across major languages, additional Fireworks AI and NVIDIA Nemotron models, and third-party guardrails from Palo Alto and Zenity.

  • Source: Microsoft AI Foundry Blog

item.title: github: Ask Copilot in security assessments now available
item.description:
GitHub added an Ask Copilot entry point in security assessment results, letting organization admins and security managers get contextual explanations and next-step guidance directly from secret risk assessment and Code Security risk assessment views.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: ai: Foundry Local is now Generally Available
item.description:
Microsoft says Foundry Local is now generally available for running AI inference on-device. The announcement emphasizes local deployment, privacy, and no per-token costs.

  • Source: Microsoft AI Foundry Blog

item.title: security: The agentic SOC—Rethinking SecOps for the next decade
item.description:
Microsoft describes a future SOC model where autonomous defense handles machine-speed response, agents provide context and coordination, and human analysts focus on judgment, risk, and outcomes.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: New Low Quality option in the Hide comment menu
item.description:
GitHub moderators can now mark hidden comments as Low Quality from the Hide comment menu. The option is available on issues, discussions, pull requests, and commits.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: New Sort by control added to Notifications
item.description:
GitHub Notifications now includes a new “Sort by” control with two options: “Newest to oldest” and “Oldest to newest.” The default behavior remains “Newest to oldest.”

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Repository member role labels now in pull request list view
item.description:
GitHub now shows repository member role labels inline in the pull request list view for public repositories. Labels such as First-time contributor, Contributor, and Member appear directly in the list.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Release information in issue sidebar and default values for project fields
item.description:
GitHub added release tracking details to the issue sidebar, configurable default values for project fields, and faster navigation between related issues.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: Investigating Storm-2755: “Payroll pirate” attacks targeting Canadian employees
item.description:
Microsoft reports on Storm-2755, a financially motivated threat actor targeting Canadian employee accounts to access profile data and redirect salary payments to attacker-controlled accounts. The post is based on observations from Microsoft Incident Response – Detection and Response Team (DART).

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: Intent redirection vulnerability in third-party SDK exposed millions of Android wallets to potential risk
item.description:
Microsoft says a severe Android intent-redirection flaw in a widely used third-party SDK exposed sensitive user data across millions of apps, including Android wallets. The post explains the issue, its risk, and recommends updating affected SDKs to reduce exposure.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: aks: Release 2026-04-02
item.description:
AKS release 2026-04-02 adds new Kubernetes patch versions, preview and GA feature updates, component bumps, and several bug fixes/security remediations. It also announces future retirements for Ubuntu 22.04, a kubelet certificate rotation disable tag, and Teleport (preview).

  • Source: Release notes from AKS

item.title: github: Copilot-reviewed pull request merge metrics now in the usage metrics API
item.description:
The Copilot usage metrics API now includes two new merge metrics for Copilot-reviewed pull requests, extending the pull request throughput and cycle-time metrics added in February. These metrics track PRs reviewed by Copilot from the coding agent.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Secret scanning improvements to alert APIs, webhooks, and delegated workflows
item.description:
GitHub is rolling out updates to secret scanning alert APIs, webhooks, and delegated workflows. The changes are aimed at improving the developer experience for secret scanning features.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Mobile: Research and code with Copilot cloud agent anywhere
item.description:
GitHub Mobile now supports Copilot cloud agent beyond pull request workflows, letting users continue work away from their desk. The update adds the ability to ask Copilot to research and code from mobile.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Enhancements to maintenance notifications in Azure Service Health
item.description:
Azure Service Health now provides regional, consolidated maintenance notifications for Azure Database for PostgreSQL. Instead of separate notices for each server, customers receive one notification per region for upcoming maintenance.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: PgBouncer 1.25.1 support in Azure Database for PostgreSQL – Flexible Server
item.description:
PgBouncer 1.25.1 is now generally available in Azure Database for PostgreSQL – Flexible Server. It is offered as a built-in connection pooling feature to help manage idle and short-lived connections with low overhead.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Files assessments are now available using Azure Migrate
item.description:
Azure Migrate now supports public preview assessments for Azure Files. It can discover on-premises SMB and NFS file shares and provide migration planning for Windows and Linux shares to Azure Files.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Observability in AKS Namespace and Workload Views
item.description:
AKS Namespace and Workload views now include observability data from Azure Monitor managed service for Prometheus. This adds cluster and workload health, troubleshooting, and resource utilization details directly in those views.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Code Security risk assessment available for organizations
item.description:
Organization admins and security managers can now run a free Code Security risk assessment for their organization. It summarizes vulnerabilities by severity, rule type, and related categories.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: New PGP signing key for GitHub CLI Linux packages
item.description:
GitHub CLI’s Linux package repositories now use an updated PGP keyring that includes both the existing signing key and a replacement key. The change is meant to support package signing continuity and will matter if you refresh or verify repository keys.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio Code, March Releases
item.description:
VS Code moved to weekly stable releases, and this changelog covers Copilot-related updates in VS Code versions 1.111 through 1.115 shipped in March and early April 2026. The post says the highlights include Autopilot for fully automated workflows, but the provided excerpt does not include the detailed release items.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Red Hat OpenShift adds NVIDIA H100 and H200 GPU support
item.description:
Azure Red Hat OpenShift (ARO) now supports NVIDIA H100 and H200 GPU-based Azure VM SKUs. The update targets large-scale AI, machine learning, and HPC workloads on the managed OpenShift service.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: dotnet: Your Migration’s Source of Truth: The Modernization Assessment
item.description:
The post introduces GitHub Copilot Modernization and focuses on the assessment report as the source of truth for cloud modernization work. It frames the report as part of the migration process and positions it as the primary reference for modernization planning.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Disable HTTP proxy in AKS
item.description:
Azure Kubernetes Service now supports disabling HTTP proxy settings in an existing cluster. This helps administrators remove or change proxy configuration as network requirements evolve without needing disruptive cluster changes.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Functions now supports MCP resource triggers
item.description:
Azure Functions now generally supports MCP resource triggers for MCP servers hosted on Functions. This lets developers expose resources directly, in addition to tools.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Pod CIDR expansion in AKS
item.description:
Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) now supports generally available Pod CIDR expansion for overlay-based clusters. This lets teams increase the pod IP range when clusters run out of addresses, avoiding cluster rebuilds.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: dotnet: ASP.NET Core 2.3 end of support announcement
item.description:
ASP.NET Core 2.3 will reach end of support on April 7, 2027. Microsoft says users should plan to upgrade applications to a currently supported .NET version.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: github: Prioritize security alerts with runtime context from Dynatrace
item.description:
GitHub Advanced Security alerts can now be prioritized using runtime context from Dynatrace, including deployed artifacts and runtime risk in Kubernetes environments. This adds environment-aware signal to help rank alerts.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot CLI now supports BYOK and local models
item.description:
GitHub Copilot CLI now supports connecting your own model provider (BYOK) or using fully local models instead of GitHub-hosted model routing. The change gives CLI users more control over where models run and which providers they use.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Dependabot alerts are now assignable to AI agents for remediation
item.description:
Dependabot alerts can now be assigned to AI coding agents for remediation, including Copilot, Claude, and Codex. This is intended for vulnerabilities that require code changes beyond a simple dependency version update.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Dependabot version updates now support the Nix ecosystem
item.description:
Dependabot version updates now support the Nix ecosystem via Nix flakes. You can add nix as a package ecosystem in dependabot.yml and Dependabot will monitor flake.lock inputs and open pull requests when updates are available.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: SOHO router compromise leads to DNS hijacking and adversary-in-the-middle attacks
item.description:
Microsoft says Forest Blizzard, a threat actor linked to the Russian military, is compromising insecure home and small-office routers and changing their settings to support malicious infrastructure. The activity includes DNS hijacking and adversary-in-the-middle attacks.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Code scanning: Batch apply security alert suggestions on pull requests
item.description:
GitHub code scanning alerts on pull requests can now be fixed in bulk from the Files changed tab. The update lets users apply multiple security alert suggestions at once instead of handling them individually.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: npm trusted publishing now supports CircleCI
item.description:
npm trusted publishing now supports CircleCI as an OIDC provider for publishing packages to npm. Maintainers can authenticate from CircleCI workflows without stored credentials, alongside existing support for GitHub Actions and GitLab CI/CD.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot usage metrics now identify active and passive Copilot code review users
item.description:
GitHub Copilot usage metrics now show which users have Copilot code review activity and whether that activity was active or passive. Enterprise and organization admins can use these metrics to see how users engage with Copilot code review.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Network Security Perimeter for Azure Service Bus
item.description:
Network Security Perimeter support for Azure Service Bus is now generally available. It lets you define a logical network boundary around Service Bus namespaces and other Azure PaaS resources to block unauthorized public access.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: security: Inside an AI‑enabled device code phishing campaign
item.description:
Microsoft describes a new device code phishing campaign that uses AI and end-to-end automation to generate live authentication codes on demand. The result is more scalable account compromise, higher success rates, and longer post-compromise access.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Rule impact analysis on Azure Network Watcher
item.description:
Azure Network Watcher now offers rule impact analysis in public preview. It lets you preview how security admin rules will affect your environment before applying them, including validating behavior and identifying possible conflicts.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: security: Storm-1175 focuses gaze on vulnerable web-facing assets in high-tempo Medusa ransomware operations
item.description:
Microsoft says Storm-1175 is running fast-moving Medusa ransomware campaigns that exploit recently disclosed vulnerabilities in web-facing systems for initial access, data theft, and ransomware deployment. The actor uses Medusa ransomware, also referred to as Gaze.exe.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Organization runner controls for Copilot cloud agent
item.description:
GitHub added organization-level runner controls for Copilot cloud agent development environments. Copilot cloud agent tasks now start on GitHub Actions, and orgs can control which runners are used instead of relying only on the default GitHub-hosted runner.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GPT-5.1 Codex, GPT-5.1-Codex-Max, and GPT-5.1-Codex-Mini deprecated
item.description:
GitHub deprecated GPT-5.1 Codex, GPT-5.1-Codex-Max, and GPT-5.1-Codex-Mini across all GitHub Copilot experiences on April 1, 2026. The change applies to Copilot Chat, inline edits, ask and agent modes, and code completions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Organization firewall settings for Copilot cloud agent
item.description:
GitHub added organization-level firewall settings for Copilot cloud agent. The built-in agent firewall can now be configured centrally to control internet access and help reduce prompt injection and data exfiltration risk.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot cloud agent signs its commits
item.description:
Copilot cloud agent now signs every commit it creates. These commits show up as Verified on GitHub, indicating they were made by the agent and not altered after signing.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot SDK in public preview
item.description:
GitHub Copilot SDK is now in public preview. It provides building blocks to embed Copilot’s agentic capabilities into applications, workflows, and platform services.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot usage metrics now includes per-user GitHub Copilot CLI activity in organization reports
item.description:
GitHub Copilot organization reports now include per-user GitHub Copilot CLI activity. This completes CLI metrics coverage across enterprise-, user-, and organization-level reporting.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Actions: Early April 2026 updates
item.description:
GitHub Actions added entrypoint and command overrides for service containers. The update also includes new security features: OIDC custom properties and VNET failover.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: dotnet: Explore union types in C# 15
item.description:
C# 15 adds preview support for union types, letting you define a closed set of case types with implicit conversions and exhaustive pattern matching. The post also notes this fits into a broader exhaustiveness roadmap.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: vscode: Insiders (version 1.111)
item.description:
The provided content only includes the title and a link, without any release note details to summarize.

  • Source: Visual Studio Code

item.title: azure: Retirement: Python support for Azure App Service and Azure Functions on Windows will be retired on March 31, 2027
item.description:
Python apps hosted on Azure App Service on Windows and Azure Functions on Windows will be retired on March 31, 2027. After that date, app configuration and content remain, but the apps will stop running.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: AzureDnsEndpoints for Azure storage accounts will be retired March 2027
item.description:
AzureDnsZone endpoints (preview) for Azure Blob storage accounts will be retired in March 2027. Microsoft advises moving all new and existing blob storage account deployments to Standard endpoints.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: security: Threat actor abuse of AI accelerates from tool to cyberattack surface
item.description:
Microsoft says threat actors are increasingly using generative AI to improve cyberattacks, including much higher phishing click-through rates and industrialized MFA bypass. The post frames AI as part of the attack surface, not just a tool attackers use.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: Cookie-controlled PHP webshells: A stealthy tradecraft in Linux hosting environments
item.description:
Microsoft describes a Linux-hosting intrusion technique where PHP webshells are triggered by specially crafted HTTP cookies. The post covers obfuscation, php-fpm execution, and cron-based persistence used to hide activity and maintain access.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio — March update
item.description:
GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio got extensibility updates in the March 2026 release, including custom agents, agent skills, and new tools that improve agent capabilities.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: The Security tab is now Security & quality
item.description:
GitHub has renamed the top-level Security tab to Security & quality across repositories, organizations, and enterprises on github.com. The navigation now groups code quality findings together with security-related information.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Improved search for GitHub Issues is now generally available
item.description:
GitHub says improved search for Issues is now generally available. It was previously in public preview and later added to the Issues dashboard.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot organization custom instructions are generally available
item.description:
GitHub Copilot organization custom instructions are now generally available. Copilot Business and Copilot Enterprise org admins can set default instructions for their organization.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: Retirement: Service Fabric support for Windows Server 2022 ends on March 31, 2027 - upgrade to Windows Server 2025
item.description:
Service Fabric support for clusters running on Windows Server 2022 ends on March 31, 2027. Customers need to move to Windows Server 2025 before then to stay supported.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: security: Mitigating the Axios npm supply chain compromise
item.description:
Microsoft reports that Axios was compromised in a March 31, 2026 npm supply chain attack. Two newly published version-update packages were used to download from command-and-control infrastructure, which Microsoft Threat Intelligence attributes to the North Korean actor Sapphire Sleet.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: azure: Retirement: Service Fabric support for Windows Server 2019 ends on March 31, 2027 - upgrade to Windows Server 2025
item.description:
Service Fabric support for clusters running on Windows Server 2019 ends on March 31, 2027. Customers need to upgrade to Windows Server 2025 by then to stay supported.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Unlock Client-Side Configuration at Scale with Azure App Configuration and Azure Front Door
item.description:
Azure App Configuration now integrates with Azure Front Door in public preview, allowing dynamic configuration to be delivered directly to client-side applications at CDN scale. The update focuses on secure distribution and new flexibility for modern app configurations.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Research, plan, and code with Copilot cloud agent
item.description:
Copilot cloud agent (formerly known as Copilot coding agent) is no longer limited to pull-request workflows, unlocking a broader range of ways to put Copilot to work. More control over…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Data Box enhances compliance with automatic Secure Erasure Certificates
item.description:
Azure Data Box now automatically provides a downloadable Secure Erasure Certificate for each completed order. The certificate verifies the device data was securely erased according to NIST 800-88 Revision 2.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In development] Public Preview: Azure NetApp Files storage with cool access enhancement
item.description:
Azure NetApp Files adds a QoS update for cool access on Premium and Ultra service levels. Throughput now adjusts automatically as data moves to cool storage, helping balance performance and cost for mixed hot and cool workloads.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Data Box now supports Azure Files Provisioned v2
item.description:
Azure Data Box now supports ingesting data into Azure Files Provisioned v2 storage accounts. Azure Files Provisioned v2 lets customers provision and pay for specific capacity, IOPS, and throughput.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Select Azure AI Language Features
item.description:
Microsoft will retire eight Azure AI Language features on March 20, 2027. The notice lists Key Phrase Extraction, Sentiment Analysis and Opinion Mining, Custom Text Classification, Conversational Language Understanding (CLU), Custom Question Answering (CQA), and other related language features.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Codespaces is now generally available for GitHub Enterprise with data residency
item.description:
GitHub Codespaces is now generally available for GitHub Enterprise Cloud with data residency. Enterprises can use cloud development environments with configurable security while keeping data residency requirements in place.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GPT-5.4 mini is now available in Copilot Student auto model selection
item.description:
GPT-5.4 mini is now generally available to Copilot Student users through Copilot auto model selection. It is included in Auto for GitHub Copilot Chat in supported clients such as Visual Studio Code and Visual Studio.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Mobile: Stay in flow with a refreshed Copilot tab and native session logs
item.description:
GitHub Mobile now includes a refreshed Copilot tab, native session logs, and additional in-app controls for agent sessions. The update is aimed at making it easier to manage agentic workflows from the mobile app.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Mobile: Faster, more flexible agent assignment from issues
item.description:
GitHub Mobile adds an Assign an Agent option in the issue overflow menu, making it faster to delegate work from an issue. The update is focused on quicker access to agent assignment and more flexible delegation from mobile.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: devops+ai: 🔥Agentic AI Email Bot on AWS Bedrock AgentCore: Full Architecture and O365 Integration (1/2)🔥
item.description:
The page is an article introducing an agentic AI email bot built on AWS Bedrock AgentCore with Microsoft O365 integration. The provided excerpt is only a brief tagline and does not include implementation details.

  • Source: Let’s do DevOps
